analysis: 3D convolution has 8 nested loops - O(N*D*H*W*K*T*R*S*C) complexity
No im2col/implicit GEMM transformation: cannot leverage tensor core MMA units
Boundary checks (if dd >= 0...) executed per element cause massive branch divergence
Each element loaded once then discarded; filter window reuse = 0
Memory access pattern non-coalesced: threads access different spatial locations
schedule_reasoning: Conv3d fprop transforms to implicit GEMM: M=N*D'*H'*W', N=K, K=T*R*S*C where D',H',W' are output spatial dims
Optimized iterator precomputes offset table: eliminates per-element coordinate math during inner loop
128x128 tile optimal for Ampere: 4 warps per tile, each warp computes 64x64 using 16x8x16 MMA
3 stages with 32-deep K-tile: 24KB smem fits default carveout, 2-stage async prefetch hides memory latency
NDHWC layout keeps channels contiguous: enables vectorized 8-element loads (align8)
Unlike 2D conv, 3D adds depth dimension T - filter window traverses D, so K-dimension = T*R*S*C is larger
optimized_kernel:
// CUTLASS Conv3d Fprop - Optimized iterator, f16 with f32 accumulator
using cutlass_tensorop_s16816fprop3d_optimized_f16_128x128_32x3_base =
typename cutlass::conv::kernel::DefaultConv3dFprop<
    cutlass::half_t,                             // Input type
    cutlass::layout::TensorNDHWC,                // Input layout (5D)
    cutlass::half_t,                             // Filter type  
    cutlass::layout::TensorNDHWC,                // Filter layout
    float,                                       // Output type
    cutlass::layout::TensorNDHWC,                // Output layout
    float,                                       // Accumulator
    cutlass::arch::OpClassTensorOp,              // Tensor core operation
    cutlass::arch::Sm80,                         // Ampere architecture
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<128, 128, 32>,      // Threadblock tile
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<64, 64, 32>,        // Warp tile
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<16, 8, 16>,         // MMA instruction shape
    cutlass::epilogue::thread::LinearCombination<
        float, 4, float, float                   // C = alpha*A*B + beta*C
    >,
    cutlass::gemm::threadblock::GemmIdentityThreadblockSwizzle<4>,
    3,                                           // Pipeline stages
    cutlass::arch::OpMultiplyAdd,
    cutlass::conv::IteratorAlgorithm::kOptimized, // Precomputed offsets
    cutlass::conv::StrideSupport::kStrided       // Support non-unit stride
>::Kernel;
concept: Conv3d Fprop - 3D volumetric convolution as implicit GEMM

constraints.special: A is lower triangular, unit diagonal, right side multiplication

analysis: Per-element branch (if k > col) causes massive warp divergence
~50% of A elements are zero (above diagonal) but we still load and multiply
No tile-level optimization: tiles fully above diagonal should be skipped entirely
Unit diagonal not handled efficiently: could skip loads for k==col
SideMode and FillMode not considered at algorithm level
schedule_reasoning: TRMM is B*A where A is triangular - SideMode::kRight handles this without explicit transposition
FillMode::kLower tells CUTLASS A's upper triangle is zero - tile scheduler skips those regions entirely
DiagType::kUnit avoids loading diagonal elements - implicit 1.0 multiplication saves memory bandwidth
TF32 MMA (16x8x8) provides 8x speedup over FMA while maintaining f32 precision for scientific computing
128x256 tile is wide to match B's column-major layout: consecutive threads access consecutive columns
Align1 allows arbitrary matrix dimensions without padding requirements
optimized_kernel:
// CUTLASS TRMM - Right side, Lower triangular, Unit diagonal
using Operation_trmm_right_lower_unit = typename cutlass::gemm::device::Trmm<
    float, cutlass::layout::ColumnMajor,         // A (triangular matrix)
    cutlass::SideMode::kRight,                   // D = B * A (multiply from right)
    cutlass::FillMode::kLower,                   // Lower triangular
    cutlass::DiagType::kUnit,                    // Implicit 1s on diagonal
    float, cutlass::layout::ColumnMajor,         // B
    float, cutlass::layout::ColumnMajor,         // D (output)
    float,                                       // Compute type
    cutlass::arch::OpClassTensorOp,              // Tensor core operation
    cutlass::arch::Sm80,                         // Ampere
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<128, 256, 16>,      // Threadblock tile
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<64, 64, 16>,        // Warp tile
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<16, 8, 8>,          // TF32 MMA instruction
    cutlass::epilogue::thread::LinearCombination<
        float, 1, float, float,
        cutlass::epilogue::thread::ScaleType::OnlyAlphaScaling  // D = alpha * B * A
    >,
    cutlass::gemm::threadblock::GemmIdentityThreadblockSwizzle<8>,
    3,                                           // Pipeline stages
    1, 1,                                        // Alignment A, B
    false,                                       // Split-K disabled
    cutlass::arch::OpMultiplyAddFastF32          // TF32 tensor ops
>;
concept: TRMM - Triangular matrix multiply with structure-aware scheduling

constraints.special: C = alpha*A*A^T + beta*C, C is symmetric (upper fill)

analysis: row <= col check means ~50% of threads in lower triangle do nothing - massive underutilization
A is loaded twice: once for row access, once for col access (A^T) - no sharing
Tile scheduler doesn't know about symmetry: computes both (i,j) and (j,i) tiles unnecessarily
Diagonal tiles are partially empty, causing divergence within warps
Rank-K structure (A*A^T) not exploited: same matrix appears twice but loaded independently
schedule_reasoning: SYRK computes C = A*A^T - FillMode::kUpper tells CUTLASS to only compute upper triangle tiles
Tile scheduler skips tiles entirely below diagonal: O(N^2) tiles → O(N^2/2) tiles
Square 128x128 tile: diagonal tiles are on-diagonal, easy symmetry handling
5 pipeline stages with K=16: high latency hiding for the K-dimension reduction
A matrix loaded once, shared between A and A^T access via transposed shared memory bank
Align4 enables 128-bit loads: 4 floats per transaction, maximizing memory bandwidth
optimized_kernel:
// CUTLASS SYRK - Symmetric Rank-K update, upper fill
using Operation_syrk_upper = typename cutlass::gemm::device::RankK<
    float, cutlass::layout::RowMajor,            // A (N x K)
    float, cutlass::layout::ColumnMajor,         // C (N x N, symmetric)
    cutlass::FillMode::kUpper,                   // Store only upper triangle
    float,                                       // Compute type
    cutlass::arch::OpClassTensorOp,              // Tensor core operation
    cutlass::arch::Sm80,                         // Ampere
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<128, 128, 16>,      // Threadblock tile
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<64, 64, 16>,        // Warp tile
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<16, 8, 8>,          // TF32 MMA instruction
    cutlass::epilogue::thread::LinearCombination<
        float, 1, float, float                   // C = alpha*A*A^T + beta*C
    >,
    cutlass::gemm::threadblock::GemmIdentityThreadblockSwizzle<8>,
    5,                                           // Pipeline stages (deep for K-reduction)
    4,                                           // Alignment (128-bit loads)
    false,                                       // Split-K disabled
    cutlass::arch::OpMultiplyAddFastF32          // TF32 tensor ops
>;
concept: SYRK - Symmetric rank-k update exploiting output symmetry

constraints.special: C = alpha*A*B + beta*C, A is symmetric (lower fill), left side

analysis: Per-element branch (row >= k) causes warp divergence on every element
Symmetric access A[k,row] when row < k creates irregular memory pattern
Cache lines loaded for lower triangle may be evicted before upper triangle access needs them
No tile-level symmetry: each tile handles symmetry element-by-element
Doesn't recognize that A*B where A is symmetric can be computed more efficiently
schedule_reasoning: SYMM with SideMode::kLeft computes C = A*B where A is symmetric
FillMode::kLower: CUTLASS reads lower triangle and internally mirrors to upper
Tile-level handling: when loading A tile, loader fetches mirrored elements for upper portion
128x128 tile with K=32: deep K for better arithmetic intensity
4 stages @ 8KB each = 32KB smem: fits within default carveout
ColMajor layout for all matrices: standard Fortran/LAPACK convention for BLAS
optimized_kernel:
// CUTLASS SYMM - Symmetric matrix multiply, left side, lower fill
using Operation_symm_left_lower = typename cutlass::gemm::device::Symm<
    float, cutlass::layout::ColumnMajor,         // A (M x M, symmetric)
    cutlass::SideMode::kLeft,                    // C = A * B (A on left)
    cutlass::FillMode::kLower,                   // A stores lower triangle
    float, cutlass::layout::ColumnMajor,         // B (M x N)
    float, cutlass::layout::ColumnMajor,         // C (M x N)
    float,                                       // Compute type
    cutlass::arch::OpClassTensorOp,              // Tensor core operation
    cutlass::arch::Sm80,                         // Ampere
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<128, 128, 32>,      // Threadblock tile
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<64, 64, 32>,        // Warp tile
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<16, 8, 8>,          // TF32 MMA instruction
    cutlass::epilogue::thread::LinearCombination<
        float, 4, float, float                   // C = alpha*A*B + beta*C
    >,
    cutlass::gemm::threadblock::GemmIdentityThreadblockSwizzle<8>,
    4,                                           // Pipeline stages
    1, 4,                                        // Alignment A, B
    false,                                       // Split-K disabled
    cutlass::arch::OpMultiplyAddFastF32          // TF32 tensor ops
>;
concept: SYMM - Symmetric matrix multiply with structure-aware A access

constraints.special: Complex numbers stored as separate real/imag arrays (planar layout)

analysis: Interleaved (AoS) layout: real/imag adjacent in memory prevents efficient vectorized loads
Complex multiply requires 4 real multiplies + 2 adds per pair - no MMA utilization
Tensor cores operate on real matrices; interleaved complex doesn't map to MMA
Each load fetches (real, imag) pair but tensor core wants pure real or pure imag tiles
Cannot batch real*real and imag*imag operations for efficient scheduling
schedule_reasoning: Planar layout stores real[] and imag[] as separate contiguous arrays
This allows tensor cores to operate on pure-real tiles: C_real += A_real*B_real - A_imag*B_imag
CUTLASS schedules 4 real GEMMs internally: RR, RI, IR, II then combines in epilogue
ComplexTransform::kConjugate on both matrices handles C = conj(A) * conj(B) patterns common in signal processing
128x64 tile: asymmetric to balance smem between A and B given planar doubling
Align8 enables 128-bit vector loads: 8 half values per transaction
optimized_kernel:
// CUTLASS Planar Complex GEMM - f16 with separate real/imag arrays
using Operation_planar_complex_gemm = typename cutlass::gemm::kernel::DefaultGemmPlanarComplexUniversal<
    cutlass::half_t, cutlass::layout::RowMajor,  // A real/imag arrays
    cutlass::ComplexTransform::kConjugate, 8,    // Transform A, alignment
    cutlass::half_t, cutlass::layout::RowMajor,  // B real/imag arrays
    cutlass::ComplexTransform::kConjugate, 8,    // Transform B, alignment
    cutlass::half_t, cutlass::layout::RowMajor,  // C real/imag arrays
    float,                                       // Accumulator (real)
    cutlass::arch::OpClassTensorOp,              // Tensor core operation
    cutlass::arch::Sm80,                         // Ampere
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<128, 64, 32>,       // Threadblock tile
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<32, 32, 32>,        // Warp tile
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<16, 8, 16>,         // f16 MMA instruction
    cutlass::epilogue::thread::LinearCombinationPlanarComplex<
        cutlass::half_t, 8, float, float         // Complex alpha, beta support
    >,
    cutlass::gemm::threadblock::GemmIdentityThreadblockSwizzle<>,
    3,                                           // Pipeline stages
    cutlass::arch::OpMultiplyAdd                 // f16 tensor op
>::GemmKernel;
concept: Planar Complex GEMM - tensor core complex arithmetic via real/imag separation
